page_title: State income tax and unemployment insurance — Delaware
target_keywords: delaware de-w4 form vs federal w-4

sources.title: Delaware Division of Revenue — Tax Rate Changes (Title 30 § 1102 rate ladder)
sources.note: Authoritative for the seven-bracket structure 0%/2.2%/3.9%/4.8%/5.2%/5.55%/6.6% and base-tax columns

sources.title: Delaware Employer's Guide — Withholding Regulations and Employer's Duties
sources.note: Authoritative for lookback period 1 Jul-30 Jun, W-1Q/W-1/W-1A thresholds ($6,020 / $33,460), Form W-3 annual reconciliation, OneStop Delaware registration, EFT requirement under IRC § 6302

sources.title: Delaware Department of Labor — Unemployment Insurance Employer Services
sources.note: 2026 new-employer rate 1.0% (down from 1.2% in 2025), experience range 0.3%-5.4%, $14,500 wage base, UC-8/UC-8A quarterly forms, Title 19 Ch. 33 authority, April rate-notice cadence

sources.title: Bloomberg Tax — Delaware Unemployment Insurance Tax Schedule Unchanged for 2026
sources.note: Schedule B confirmed for 2026, experienced range 0.4%-5.4% (DE DOL says 0.3% floor), delinquent rate 6.3%, 0.2% Operations & Technology Assessment now billed separately quarterly

sources.title: EY Tax News — 2026 State Unemployment Insurance Taxable Wage Bases
sources.note: Confirms $14,500 wage base for 2026 (up $2,000 from $12,500 in 2025) per HB 433 phase-in

sources.title: Experian Employer Services — Legislative Update: Delaware HB 433
sources.note: HB 433 (2024) phase-in schedule: $12,500 (2025) → $14,500 (2026) → $16,500 (2027), Benefit Ratio methodology effective 2027

sources.title: Delaware Division of Revenue — DE-W4 FAQ
sources.note: DE-W4 form authority — Delaware created its own state W-4 because it kept the allowance-count model after the federal redesign removed allowances in 2020

sources.title: Delaware Division of Revenue — Doing Business in Delaware
sources.note: Confirms 8.7% Delaware corporate income tax rate (Title 30 § 1903), distinct from employment withholding statute under Title 30 Chapter 11

sources.title: Delaware Code Title 30 Chapter 11 Subchapter VII — Withholding of Tax
sources.note: Primary statute for Delaware employer withholding obligation, § 1151 onwards

sources.title: Delaware General Assembly — House Bill 433 detail
sources.note: Bill text and history for HB 433 (2024) — wage-base phase-in plus shift to Benefit Ratio rate methodology effective 2027

sources.title: Hero photo — Wilmington, Delaware (Sarah Mason, Unsplash)
sources.note: 134 KB JPEG, real photography, Unsplash editorial licence with attribution to @sarahthemason

internal_links_used.anchor: See when EOR stops being right / EOR vs Entity Crossover Calculator

internal_links_pending.anchor: Hiring in the United States, overview

high_concern_flags.detail: Row brief said 'wage base around $10,500-$12,500' — verified actual 2026 wage base is $14,500 per HB 433 phase-in (EY Tax News, DE Bill, Experian). $10,500 was 2024 pre-HB 433 baseline; $12,500 was 2025. Row brief also said new-employer rate '~1.5%-2.0%' — actual 2026 rate is 1.0% (Bloomberg Tax + DE DOL). Used verified numbers.

UI experience-rated floor: 0.3% per DE DOL employer-services page vs 0.4% per Bloomberg Tax (April 2026). Page uses 0.3% (DE DOL is the more authoritative state source). 0.1 percentage point spread; ceiling of 5.4% is consistent across sources.
3 internal links rendered as plain text because target pages not yet live: US parent overview, Delaware state overview, Delaware worker-classification child
